BASS: multi-scale and multi-sample analysis enables accurate cell type clustering and spatial domain detection in spatial transcriptomic studies
reason: Bayesian hierarchical model jointly infers cell-type clusters and spatial domains across multiple spatial transcriptomics samples.

title: Identification of spatially variable genes with graph cuts
reason: HMRF and graph-cut formulation provides a discrete graph-optimized alternative for spatially variable gene detection.

title: SOMDE: a scalable method for identifying spatially variable genes with self-organizing map
reason: Self-organizing-map compression anticipates modern spatial tokenization and graph coarsening ideas for scalable SVG testing.

title: Contrastive and Adaptive Multi-modal Masked Autoencoder for Spatial Transcriptomics
reason: Anchor-guided multimodal masked autoencoder for whole-slide spatial transcriptomics imputation using partial gene-expression measurements.

title: HiST: A Hierarchical Sparse Transformer for Cross-Modal Spatial Transcriptomics Modeling
reason: Sparse hierarchical transformer for histology-to-ST prediction that scales with active measured locations rather than dense slide area.

title: Do Foundation Models See Biology? Evaluating Attention Coherence with Spatial Transcriptomics in Glioblastoma
reason: Uses co-registered spatial transcriptomics to quantitatively evaluate biological coherence of pathology foundation-model attention maps.

title: GC-MoE: Genomics-Guided Cell-Type-Specific Mixture of Experts for Histology-Based Single-Cell Spatial Transcriptomics
reason: Cell-type-routed mixture of experts with co-expression prediction and neighborhood attention for single-cell spatial expression inference from histology.

title: DriftST: One-Step Generative Inference of Spatial Transcriptomics from H&E Histology
reason: One-step generative histology-to-ST inference using Cellular Drifting, gene co-expression attention, and gene residual gates.

title: DiSTILL: A Hybrid Cloud-HPC Workflow System for Reproducible Spatial Transcriptomics Analysis
reason: Workflow infrastructure for reproducible spatial transcriptomics analyses across cloud and HPC execution environments.

title: JASPR: Joint Spatial Representation learning of histology and spatial genomics for improved virtual genomic screening and clinical prognostication
reason: Self-supervised joint spatial representation learning of histology and spatial genomics using cross-modal reconstruction.

title: Consistent Bayesian Local Spatial Feature Selection with Application to Spatial Multimodal Omics
reason: Bayesian local feature and basis selection over contiguous spatial partitions for interpretable spatial multimodal omics.

title: Multi-resolution Spatial Graphical Regression Models for Hierarchical Spatial Transcriptomics Data
reason: Bayesian multi-resolution spatial graphical regression for spatially varying gene regulatory networks.

title: Spatially informed reference-free cell-type deconvolution for spatial transcriptomics with SpatialCD
reason: Reference-free LDA-style deconvolution with spatial graph regularization, ADMM inference, and data-driven cell-type-number selection.

title: SPADE: Spatial transcriptomics and pathology alignment using a mixture of data experts for an expressive latent space
reason: ST-informed pathology foundation model with mixture-of-data-experts contrastive alignment between H&E patches and gene-expression embeddings.

title: Riemannian metric learning for alignment of spatial multiomics
reason: Manifold Gromov-Wasserstein learns modality-specific Riemannian pullback metrics with neural fields for spatial multiomics alignment.

title: SpaCellAgent: A Self-Evolving LLM-Based Multi-Agent Framework for Trajectory Analysis
reason: LLM multi-agent system for automated trajectory inference and spatiotemporal analysis in single-cell and spatial transcriptomics.
